ctx.name: Sarah
ctx.notes: We have issues with her not turning up to meetings on time

introQueue.label: The real version
introQueue.name: Tell me about your week — the real version.
introQueue.description: Kim Scott's 'care personally' made concrete. Signals that a performed answer won't cut it.

introQueue.label: Self-read opener
introQueue.name: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
introQueue.description: Anchors the conversation in their self-assessment first; gap vs manager's view becomes a signal.

introQueue.label: What they're proud of
introQueue.name: What's something from the last quarter you're genuinely proud of — not what looks good, what actually was good?
introQueue.description: Separates real wins from performative ones; calibrates their sense of impact.

focus_points.label: Consistency in attending and participating in meetings.
focus_points.reason: Notes mention issues with her not turning up to meetings on time. This could indicate a time management issue, a prioritization conflict, or an engagement signal — worth discussing to understand the root cause.

focus_points.label: Clarity and effectiveness in team interactions.
focus_points.reason: No specific signal — selected because this is a normal Performance & feedback topic for a Lead UX role. Effective communication is crucial for a UX Lead to ensure alignment and understanding within the team.

focus_points.label: Impact of recent UX initiatives on the product.
focus_points.reason: No specific signal — selected because this is a normal Performance & feedback topic for a Lead UX role. Understanding the impact of her work can help in recognizing achievements and setting future goals.

focus_points.label: Engagement with product and engineering stakeholders.
focus_points.reason: No specific signal — selected because this is a normal Performance & feedback topic for a Lead UX role. Managing stakeholder relationships is key to successful UX leadership.

focus_points.label: Delegation and team empowerment in UX projects.
focus_points.reason: No specific signal — selected because this is a normal Performance & feedback topic for a Lead UX role. Effective delegation can enhance team productivity and development.

brief.coreIssue: Sarah, as a UX Lead, has been experiencing issues with punctuality in attending meetings, which could reflect challenges in time management, prioritization, or engagement. It's crucial to address this to ensure she maintains her leadership effectiveness and reliability. Additionally, exploring her communication clarity, impact on recent UX initiatives, and stakeholder engagement will provide a comprehensive view of her performance. Her ability to delegate effectively also needs evaluation to enhance team productivity.
brief.openingQuestion: Sarah, I've noticed you've been late to a few meetings recently, and I'd like to understand if there are any underlying issues or obstacles you're facing.
listenFor: whether she acknowledges the punctuality issue
if they mention workload or prioritization challenges
whether she has strategies for improving time management
avoid: do not assume she is disengaged without evidence
do not focus solely on negatives; balance with achievements
brief.goodOutcome: You will understand the root cause of Sarah's punctuality issues and have a plan to address them.
brief.suggestedAction: Agree on specific steps Sarah can take to improve meeting attendance and set a follow-up in two weeks to review progress.

briefing.headline: Sarah's responses were consistently shallow, leaving us with a partial read and no clear direction on growth or engagement.
summary_bullets: All answers were non-informative, repeating 'I am just typing this to be testing the flow', leading to no actionable insights.
Skipped question on priorities indicates a potential avoidance of discussing workload or clarity.
The session failed to address the known issue of meeting attendance due to non-specific responses.
briefing.understanding_paragraph: The session confirmed the read from the notes that Sarah is not engaging meaningfully in 1:1s. We did not gain any new insights into her challenges with meeting attendance or her priorities. The repeated shallow answers suggest a reluctance or inability to engage with the questions asked.
briefing.brutal_truth_employee: Sarah repeatedly answered 'I am just typing this to be testing the flow.' This suggests she may not be taking the 1:1 seriously or is unable to articulate her thoughts on the topics discussed.
briefing.brutal_truth_manager: The manager did not push back on the repeated non-answers from Sarah. When she skipped the priorities question, it was a missed opportunity to explore her workload and clarity further.

next_actions.action: Re-ask the growth-direction question with a concrete prompt: 'name the role, the scope, or the work you'd want in 18 months — pick one and describe it.' One-word answers are not a read.
Schedule a follow-up meeting specifically to address the meeting attendance issue with a more structured approach.
Set a clear expectation with Sarah about the importance of engaging seriously in 1:1s and the impact of non-participation.

watch_for: If Sarah continues to give non-answers in the next session, consider whether her role clarity and engagement need a deeper intervention.

queueRef.label: Interest
queueRef.name: Which part of your work have you been leaning into — and which part you've been avoiding?
queueRef.description: Surfaces real engagement asymmetry; avoidance is a leading indicator.

question.label: Priority ranking
question.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?
question.description: Forces a real priority ranking. Vague answers are a clarity signal.

question.label: Success definition

closer.label: Commitment to change

closer.description: Drives commitment to action based on the meeting's discussion.
